key_issues: All five iteration-001 fixes correctly and completely applied. Verdict: APPROVE (needs_revision=false).

Point 1 (weak guard) FIXED and empirically VERIFIED: per-root base-dir (path-only <root>) mandated (lines 63/30-32/87) + S1-edge teeth guard (#rx core/main.rkt or spec-2026 for the six S1-importers; #rx /(string|list).rkt$ or >=50 for tool-name) at lines 65/34-40. Confirmed by isolated probe: a wrong per-root base-dir truncates to visited=79 with core/main ABSENT even after another root loaded core/main into the process (cross-root MPI cache does not mask it), so the assertion fires RED on the real one-walk-per-root test. tool-name has no relative edges so cannot truncate; >=50 is a safe floor.

Point 2 (progress over-claim) FIXED: lines 73/76/139 add explicit 'except stdio.rkt/M5e — orphaned-until-S6a per roadmap.md:118' caveats to the :82 and :79 flips, removing the contradiction with the still-📋 :78/:87 stdio rows.
Point 3 (factor regression) FIXED: raco test mcp/core/test/ is now MANDATORY (lines 70/104/111); no 'Optionally' remains on the main-test-covering run; factored check-count box added (line 71).

Points 4 & 5 (teeth check, stdio wording) ADDED: mutation check (require racket/tcp -> RED -> revert) at lines 66/90/130; stdio reworded to 'excluded as a root' with importing-module-regression clarification at lines 67/88.

Cosmetic nits only (NOT blocking): lines 82/119 still label uri-template-test.rkt:336-341 'the non-vacuity guard' though its >1 guard is now rejected (could mislead a skimmer into copying >1, but lines 28/65/87 prevent it); line 102 one-liner understates the strengthened guard; optionally a second mutation could prove the non-vacuity guard's teeth too.
